questions.question: Which physiographic division of India is the most stable landmass?
questions.explanation: The Peninsular Plateau is composed of ancient crystalline rocks and is geologically stable.

questions.question: Which Himalayan range contains the famous valleys of Kashmir and Kangra?
questions.explanation: The Himachal range contains many famous valleys and hill stations.

questions.question: Which landform is formed by river deposition in the Northern Plains?
questions.explanation: The Northern Plains are formed by deposition of alluvial soil by rivers.

questions.question: Which relief division of the Northern Plains consists of older alluvial soil?
questions.explanation: Bhangar consists of older alluvial soil deposited earlier by rivers.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the Central Highlands?
questions.explanation: Chambal river flows through the Central Highlands region.

questions.question: Which plateau is drained by the Damodar River?
questions.explanation: The Damodar River flows through the Chotanagpur Plateau.

questions.question: Which of the following hill ranges belong to the Meghalaya Plateau?
questions.explanation: The Meghalaya Plateau includes Garo, Khasi and Jaintia Hills.

questions.question: Which mountain range lies between the Narmada and Tapi rivers?
questions.explanation: The Satpura range lies between the Narmada and Tapi rivers.

questions.question: Which river forms the largest delta on the eastern coast?
questions.explanation: The Ganga river forms the largest delta in the world.

questions.question: Which coast lies between Goa and Kerala?
questions.explanation: Kannad coast lies between Goa and Kerala.

questions.question: Which coastal plain lies between Eastern Ghats and Bay of Bengal?
questions.explanation: Eastern coastal plains lie between Eastern Ghats and Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which type of dunes are commonly found in the Indian Desert?
questions.explanation: Barchans are crescent shaped sand dunes commonly found in deserts.

questions.question: Which is the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats?
questions.explanation: Mahendragiri is the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: Which plateau is known for rich mineral deposits?
questions.explanation: Chotanagpur Plateau is rich in minerals like coal and iron.

questions.question: Which river flows westwards through the Peninsular Plateau?
questions.explanation: Narmada flows westwards into the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: Which hill station lies in the Himachal Himalayas?
questions.explanation: Shimla lies in the Himachal Himalayas.

questions.question: Which river island is the largest inhabited river island in the world?
questions.explanation: Majuli is the largest inhabited river island.

questions.question: Which of the following islands lies closest to Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: The island region near Rameswaram lies closest to Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Which feature separates India from Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: Palk Strait separates India and Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Which island group lies closer to Myanmar?
questions.explanation: Andaman and Nicobar islands lie close to Myanmar.

questions.question: Which physiographic division is known as the granary of India?
questions.explanation: The Northern Plains produce large quantities of food grains.

questions.question: Which mountain range forms the western boundary of the Peninsular Plateau?
questions.explanation: Western Ghats form the western boundary of the plateau.

questions.question: Which hill range is one of the oldest mountain ranges in India?
questions.explanation: Aravali range is one of the oldest mountain ranges.

questions.question: Which river drains the Meghalaya Plateau?
questions.explanation: Rivers from the Meghalaya Plateau drain into the Brahmaputra basin.

questions.question: Which physiographic division is mainly composed of sand dunes?
questions.explanation: The Indian Desert contains sand dunes and arid land.
